sourceText: I have made everything my own. I have made everything my own. I have made everything my own.
This phrase expresses a strong sense of ownership and dedication to one's research and inventions.
I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work.
This shows a positive and persistent attitude, viewing failure not as a setback but as part of the process towards success.
Lack of initial funding, skepticism from peers, and technical challenges in identifying the causes of diseases.
Persistent experimentation and rigorous proof, leveraging advancements in microscopy, and an unwavering commitment to truth.
Man will not die from ignorance.

This quote reflects his determination to prevent people from suffering in darkness by scientifically uncovering the causes of diseases and developing preventive and curative methods based on that knowledge.
Science is a process of constant doubt and proof.
This statement reflects his research methodology, showing his approach of not accepting anything for granted and seeking truth through rigorous experimentation and verification.
True discovery lies hidden in the unseen.
Based on his experience exploring the microbial world through a microscope, this suggests that important secrets crucial to human health are hidden within the unseen, tiny entities.
Familial opposition and imprisonment, internal dissent within his order, and overcoming complex intellectual challenges.
Through unwavering faith, relentless scholarly pursuit, and the synthesis of reason and faith, he overcame obstacles to establish his profound philosophical and theological system.
